OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Nov-16-2017 Off Calendar

Defendants Ernest Ralston. Linda Rochester And Adam Ralston'S Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand, Esq. (415-318-5709) of Hooshmand Law Group for Plaintiff.
Davin Bacho, Esq. (415-981-5411) of Bledsoe for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court explains basis for the Tentative ruling and announces the motion is frivolous on prong 2. Defendant argues. Plaintiff argues Defendant violated stipulation. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling in it's entirety rules as follows:

Defendants Ernest Ralston, Linda Rochester And Adam Ralston'S Motion And Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16 is DENIED in its entirety and plaintiffs are awarded attorneys' fees of $8,887.50 for their opposition to the motion. The moving defendants failed to satisfy their first prong burden of showing that any of the causes of action they seek to strike arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. As in Clark v. Mazgani (2009) 170 Cal. App. 4th 1281, which remains good law after Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal. 5th 376, while serving a notice to quit is protected activity, that protected activity is incidental to plaintiffs' claims. The gravamen of each of plaintiffs' claims is defendants' alleged fraudulent acts to terminate plaintiffs' tenancy based on misstatements regarding an asserted relative move-in, which is not protected activity per Clark. Even if defendants did satisfy their first prong burden, plaintiffs satisfied their second prong burden of showing that each of their claims that are sought to be stricken has minimal merit. The documents from Airbnb.com are properly authenticated and the documents from the San Francisco Office of Short-Term Rental are admissible as public records. Based on these documents as well as defendants' discovery responses, a reasonable trier of fact could find that the moving defendants are liable on each of the claims sought to be stricken. Defendants' refusal to withdraw this motion after it was made clear to them that it lacked merit, coupled with the extremely weak bases for defendants' second prong positions, shows that pursuing this motion was frivolous per CCP 128.5 and thus plaintiffs are entitled to an award of fees for opposing this motion. The amount sought by plaintiffs is reasonable. Payment of attorney's fees shall be made within 90 dyas of entry of this order. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)
